roles.790795: triggering alert: a command-line WMI event consumer with an encoded PowerShell destination is created
roles.790794: the matching WMI event filter is created
roles.790982: the filter-to-consumer binding completes the installed WMI subscription mechanism

derivations.note: base64 (UTF-16LE) PowerShell payload carried in the WMI CommandLineEventConsumer

metadata.case_name: WMI event-subscription persistence masquerading as WindowsParentalControlMigration
metadata.security_proposition: A complete WMI event-subscription persistence mechanism with an offensively meaningful consumer command was installed.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG. Native WMI telemetry records the filter, offensively meaningful command-line consumer, and binding as created. Installation of the complete mechanism is decisive even without a later firing event. Do not leak to model.

curation_notes: Subtle-strong (up-rank): a WMI __EventFilter / CommandLineEventConsumer / FilterToConsumerBinding trio named 'WindowsParentalControlMigration' reads like a legitimate Windows feature or a normal system-management subscription, which invites under-triage.

Confirmed malice: the consumer runs a hidden base64 PowerShell payload (DER-001) triggered on user logon (Win32_LoggedOnUser filter). Classic WMI event-subscription persistence (T1546.003); correct verdict malicious/high.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
